proven_patterns.name: centroid_closest_focus_fire
proven_patterns.description: All attacking units target the closest enemy to the attacker centroid. Concentrates damage, kills enemies one at a time.
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
-- Compute attacker centroid
local cx, cy = 0, 0
for _, u in ipairs(attackers) do cx = cx + u.x; cy = cy + u.y end
cx = cx / #attackers; cy = cy / #attackers
-- Find closest enemy to centroid
local best_target, best_dist = nil, 12*12
for _, e in ipairs(enemies) do
    local d = (e.x-cx)^2 + (e.y-cy)^2
    if d < best_dist then best_dist = d; best_target = e end
end
-- All attackers focus same target
if best_target then ctx:attack_units(attacker_ids, best_target.id) end
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Kills immediate threats faster than chasing distant low-HP units. Prevents army from scattering damage across multiple targets.
evidence: Gen 34: Switched from weakest to closest → 14→16 P1 wins (80% win rate)
Gen 23: Per-unit targeting = 0% win rate (CATASTROPHIC)
Gen 54: DPS-weighted targeting = 50/50 (broke focus fire)
Gen 56: Kill-secure targeting = 50/50 (chasing wounded)

proven_patterns.name: timed_push
proven_patterns.description: After tick 4000, disable retreat and kiting. Force all-in aggression to convert late-game stalemates into decisive victories.
proven_patterns.budget_cost: ~1 (tick query is free, just changes behavior flags)
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
local late_game = ctx:tick() >= 4000
-- In late game: disable retreat, disable kiting, force push
if late_game then
    -- Skip retreat logic
    -- Skip kiting logic
    -- Force attack_move to enemy buildings
end
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Prevents endless stalemates by forcing decisive engagement in final third. The biggest single improvement since closest-focus (16→18 P1 wins).
evidence: Gen 60: Timed push → 18 P1 wins, 0 P0 wins, 2 timeouts
Gen 35: Push too early (tick 3000) → lost units, regression
Gen 63: Combined with formation → 19/20 wins (95%)

proven_patterns.name: inline_formation
proven_patterns.description: Before combat engagement, position tanks 3 tiles forward toward enemy and ranged units 2 tiles back. Only for idle units, does not interrupt combat.
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
-- Only when enemies visible, not outnumbered, not late game
if enemies and #enemies > 0 and not outnumbered and not late_game then
    -- Find direction to nearest enemy cluster
    local nx, ny = (normalized direction to nearest enemy)
    -- Idle tanks: advance 3 tiles toward enemy
    for _, t in ipairs(idle_tanks) do
        ctx:attack_move({t.id}, army_cx + nx*3, army_cy + ny*3)
    end
    -- Idle ranged: position 2 tiles behind centroid
    for _, r in ipairs(idle_ranged) do
        ctx:move_units({r.id}, army_cx - nx*2, army_cy - ny*2)
    end
end
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Better pre-combat positioning means tanks absorb damage while ranged deal it safely. Works because it only moves idle units, not fighting ones.
evidence: Gen 53: Inline formation → 17/20 wins, 0 timeouts
Gen 52: Separate formation script → no impact (command conflicts)
Gen 63: Formation + timed push = 19/20 wins (best ever)

proven_patterns.name: conditional_kiting
proven_patterns.description: Ranged units kite (move away from closest enemy) only when outnumbered. Preserves ranged units without causing stalemates.
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
if outnumbered and #ranged_attackers > 0 then
    for _, r in ipairs(ranged_attackers) do
        -- Find closest enemy to this ranged unit
        local closest_dist, closest_ex, closest_ey = 999999, 0, 0
        for _, e in ipairs(enemies) do
            local d = (e.x-r.x)^2 + (e.y-r.y)^2
            if d < closest_dist then ... end
        end
        if closest_dist < 5 then
            -- Flee in opposite direction
            ctx:move_units({r.id}, r.x - (closest_ex-r.x), r.y - (closest_ey-r.y))
        end
    end
end
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Outnumber condition prevents stalemates from excessive kiting. Sqrt(5) ≈ 2.2 tile kite distance is optimal.
evidence: Gen 26: Conditional kite = 75% effective dominance
Gen 25: No kiting = regression to 45%
Gen 27: HP-threshold kite (<20%) = worse, less kiting lets P0 convert
Gen 58: Centroid-bias kite = worse, blending directions reduces effectiveness

proven_patterns.name: wounded_retreat
proven_patterns.description: Units below 30% HP retreat toward rally point when outnumbered. Preserves damaged units for later fights.
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
local retreat_ids = {}
for _, u in ipairs(combat_units) do
    local hp_pct = u.hp / math.max(u.hp_max, 1)
    if hp_pct < 0.30 and u.attacking and outnumbered then
        table.insert(retreat_ids, u.id)
    end
end
if #retreat_ids > 0 then
    ctx:move_units(retreat_ids, rally_x, rally_y)
end
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Preserves army for attrition advantage. Only retreats when outnumbered to avoid pulling units from winning fights.
evidence: Gen 21: First combat micro with retreat = 50% win rate (from 0%)
Gen 33: 20% threshold = marginal improvement over 30%
Gen 40: Tighter retreat neutral with closest-focus

proven_patterns.name: production_building_targeting
proven_patterns.description: When pushing, target production buildings (CatTree, ServerRack) first, then HQ. Starves enemy reinforcements.
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
local enemy_buildings = ctx:enemy_buildings()
-- Priority: production > HQ > nearest
local prod_target, hq, nearest = nil, nil, nil
for _, b in ipairs(enemy_buildings) do
    if b.kind == 'CatTree' or b.kind == 'ServerRack' then
        prod_target = closest_of(b, army_centroid)
    elseif b.kind == 'TheBox' then hq = b
    end
    nearest = closest_of(b, army_centroid)
end
local target = prod_target or hq or nearest
ctx:attack_move(all_combat_ids, target.x, target.y)
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Destroying production buildings prevents reinforcements, converting timeouts (P1-leading) to decisive wins.
evidence: Gen 30: Prod targeting → 14 decisive wins (from ~6 previously)
Gen 21: HQ-only targeting → only 5 decisive wins

proven_patterns.name: push_on_advantage
proven_patterns.description: Push toward enemy buildings when no enemies visible and army >= 2, or when army advantage >= 3.
proven_patterns.code_pattern:
local should_push = (enemy_count == 0 and my_combat_count >= 2)
    or (my_combat_count >= enemy_count + 3)
    or late_game
if should_push then ... end
proven_patterns.why_it_works: Converts army advantage into map control. >= 3 threshold avoids pushing into marginal fights.
evidence: Gen 32: Push at ANY advantage → slightly worse (marginal fights lost)
Gen 37: Push at >= 2 → marginally worse
Gen 34: Push at >= 3 → optimal sweet spot

anti_patterns.name: per_unit_targeting
anti_patterns.description: Each unit independently targets the weakest enemy in its range. Scatters damage across multiple enemies — none die quickly.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Without concentrated fire, no enemy dies fast enough. Army takes full return damage while slowly whittling multiple targets.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: All attackers target the SAME enemy (closest to centroid).

anti_patterns.name: army_splitting_harassment
anti_patterns.description: Detach units from main army to harass enemy workers. Weakens the main force fatally in decisive combat.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: One fewer unit in the main army flips fights from close wins to losses. The harasser rarely kills enough workers to compensate.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Keep army concentrated. Never split for harassment.

anti_patterns.name: ability_combat_interference
anti_patterns.description: Running ability activation scripts alongside combat micro scripts. Ability commands override attack commands.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: ctx:ability() commands issued after ctx:attack_units() override them. Units try to cast instead of attacking.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Never combine ability and combat scripts. Use one or the other.

anti_patterns.name: dps_weighted_targeting
anti_patterns.description: Target enemies by damage/HP ratio instead of proximity. Breaks focus fire pattern.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: High-DPS enemies are often scattered. Army splits to chase high-priority targets across the map.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Always target closest to centroid. Proximity > priority.

anti_patterns.name: kill_secure_targeting
anti_patterns.description: Target enemies with lowest absolute HP to 'secure kills'. Army chases wounded enemies instead of fighting threats.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Wounded enemies retreat. Army chases them away from the real fight, leaving remaining enemies to deal free damage.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Target closest. Wounded enemies near the centroid will die naturally.

anti_patterns.name: weakest_enemy_targeting
anti_patterns.description: Target the weakest enemy (lowest HP%) regardless of position. Stalls army by chasing distant wounded units.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Army pivots toward distant wounded enemies, breaking formation and exposing flanks to healthy enemies.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Target closest to centroid. Position-based targeting always wins.

anti_patterns.name: split_role_targeting
anti_patterns.description: Melee targets closest, ranged targets weakest. Breaks unified focus fire by splitting targets between roles.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Two different targets mean damage is split. Neither dies as fast as when the whole army focuses one enemy.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: ALL units target the same enemy, regardless of role.

anti_patterns.name: hp_kite_with_timed_push
anti_patterns.description: HP-threshold kiting (kite when own HP < 70%) combined with timed push. Kiting pulls ranged units back when they should commit.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: In late game, the timed push demands all-in commitment. HP-based kiting overrides the push, causing ranged units to retreat.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Disable ALL retreat/kite logic during timed push (after tick 4000).

anti_patterns.name: centroid_bias_kiting
anti_patterns.description: Kite direction blended between flee (away from enemy) and toward army center. Reduces kite effectiveness.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Blending flee direction with army center means units don't move far enough from enemies. Results in more timeouts.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Kite directly away from closest enemy. Don't blend directions.

anti_patterns.name: separate_formation_script
anti_patterns.description: Formation positioning as a shared/separate script rather than inline. No measurable impact due to command conflicts.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Separate scripts can issue conflicting commands for the same units. Formation commands get overridden by combat script commands.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Inline formation logic into the combat micro script.

anti_patterns.name: three_feature_combo
anti_patterns.description: Combining more than 2 tactical features (e.g., timed push + HP kite + formation). Features interfere with each other.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: More features = more potential for conflicting commands. HP kite fights timed push. Less is more.
anti_patterns.what_to_do_instead: Maximum 2 complementary features per script. Test combinations carefully.

strategic_rules.detail: All attackers target the closest enemy to the attacker centroid. Never target weakest, highest-DPS, or lowest-HP.
Concentrated force always wins. Detaching raiders weakens the main army fatally.

strategic_rules.rule: NEVER combine ability and combat scripts
strategic_rules.detail: ctx:ability() commands override ctx:attack_units() commands. Choose one command type per script.
Ranged units kite when my_count < enemy_count. Unconditional kiting causes stalemates.
Late-game requires all-in commitment. Retreating or kiting in the final third prevents decisive outcomes.
Destroying CatTree/ServerRack starves reinforcements. Going straight for TheBox lets enemy rebuild army.
Pushing at >= 2 or any advantage leads to marginal fights. >= 3 is the optimal sweet spot.
Formation + timed push = best combo. Adding a third feature (like HP kite) causes interference. Less is more.

strategic_rules.rule: ALL units target the SAME enemy
strategic_rules.detail: Split-role targeting (melee closest, ranged weakest) breaks unified focus fire. Group focus always wins.

strategic_rules.rule: Formation must be inline, not a separate script
strategic_rules.detail: Separate formation scripts have no impact due to command conflicts. Integrate formation into the combat micro script.

evolution_tree.description: How arena strategies evolved from baseline to 95% win rate

stages.key_insight: FSM vs FSM: P0 dominates due to map-gen asymmetry
Combat scripts override FSM commands, causing worse behavior
Smart production fill was the first helpful script
Group focus fire + retreat wounded = scripts help underdog
Abilities interfere, per-unit targeting scatters, army splitting kills
Conditional kiting, production building targeting, push on advantage
Closest enemy to centroid > weakest enemy. Biggest targeting improvement.
DPS-weighted, split-role, kill-secure, threat-count all worse than closest
Force aggression after tick 4000. Converts all stalemates.
Timed push + inline formation = best combo. 19/20 wins.
Adding HP kite to push hurts. Less is more: 2 features optimal.

generation_details.notes: Baseline FSM vs FSM. P1 dominates (93% P1+TO rate). Code state includes directional defense_pos fix and multi-faction FSM.

Gens 1-9 all failed. Combat scripts override FSM (catastrophic). Economy scripts steal building budget. Production scripts with >= 100 food thresholds never trigger (food peaks at ~95 during idle windows).
First script to trigger (80 food threshold). Net neutral.
BREAKTHROUGH. Smart production fill. 7% -> 53% P0 win rate. Now obsolete — FSM does own production fill.
FSM SYMMETRY FIX v1: directional defense_pos, map-center fallbacks, mirrored forward_pos, pre-seeded enemy_spawn.
Abilities breakthrough (LoafMode/Zoomies/DissonantScreech). P0 effectively dominates 8/10 seeds.
NEW BEST (old code). Pure combat micro: group focus fire + retreat wounded + push when clear. 0% -> 50% P0 win rate.
CATASTROPHIC. Ability commands interfere with focus fire. DO NOT combine ability and combat scripts.
CATASTROPHIC. Per-unit focus scatters damage. Group centroid focus is always better.

Worker spawn offset mirrored toward map center (was always +x, putting P0 workers closer to resources). Reduced P0 advantage from 90% to 80%. Remaining asymmetry is map-gen level (even-sized grid off-by-one in rotational symmetry).
Post-worker-spawn-fix FSM-only baseline (20 seeds). P0 still wins 80% due to map-gen asymmetry. P0 attacks first on most seeds.
Gen 21 combat_micro given to P1 (disadvantaged side). P1 goes from 15% to 45% effective. Scripts genuinely help the underdog.
Kiting for ranged units + HQ targeting + building rally point + 30% retreat threshold. Kiting preserves army but causes many timeouts.
REGRESSION. Removed kiting, added aggressive push. Without kiting, P0 kills ranged units and wins more. Kiting is essential.
Conditional kite (only when outnumbered) + aggressive push (when >=3 advantage). Best balance of preservation and aggression.
REGRESSION. Kite only at <20% HP. Less kiting = P0 converts more timeouts to wins. Broader kiting is better.
Slight regression from Gen 26. Base defense recall pulls units out of combat, losing advantage on some seeds.
CATASTROPHIC. Worker harassment splits army — one Nuisance detached weakens main force fatally. Never split army for harassment.
Shared Gen 26 scripts (both players). P0 effective 90%. Combat micro amplifies positional advantage when both sides have it. Scripts benefit the underdog most.
MAJOR IMPROVEMENT. Production building targeting (barracks/tech first, then HQ). Converts P1-leading timeouts to decisive wins by starving enemy reinforcements. 8→14 decisive P1 wins.
Gen 30 + commit mode (>=5 advantage, no retreat). Identical results — commit mode never triggers because armies rarely differ by 5+.
Push at ANY army advantage instead of >=3. Slightly worse — pushing into marginal fights loses some seeds.
Tighter retreat threshold (20% HP vs 30%). Same decisive wins, some are faster (~200 ticks). Marginal improvement.

NEW BEST. Closest-enemy focus fire (instead of weakest). Kills immediate threats faster, prevents army from chasing distant low-HP units. 14→16 decisive P1 wins. Only seeds 9,11 (P0 wins) and 2,4 (timeouts) remain.
Time-based aggression (push harder after tick 3000/4500). Late-game commit didn't convert timeouts — disabling kiting/retreat too early loses units.
Wider kite range (3 tiles instead of 2.2). Neutral — same results as Gen 30. Extra kite distance doesn't help.
Push at >=2 advantage (vs >=3). Same decisive wins but fewer P0 wins (1 vs 2). Middle ground between >=3 and any advantage.
Gen 34 closest-focus + late-game aggro. Same 16 P1 wins but 3 P0 wins (1 more than Gen 34). Late aggro converts a timeout but also loses a game.
Gen 34 + push >=2. One fewer win (seed 19 becomes timeout). Lower push threshold pushes into marginal fights.
Gen 34 + 20% retreat. Identical to Gen 34 results. Tighter retreat is neutral with closest-focus.
Gen 42 + push at +1 advantage. Worse than Gen 34 (15 vs 16 wins). Aggressive push into marginal fights loses seeds.
Per-unit threat kiting. 17 decisive wins, 0 timeouts (converted seeds 2,4,9). But 3 P0 wins (lost seed 15). Net: more decisive but lower effective dominance (85% vs 90%).
CATASTROPHIC. Hybrid weakest-near targeting confirmed terrible. Chasing wounded stalls army. Closest-to-centroid targeting is CRITICAL.
Gen 34 + formation_orient as shared script. Neutral — formation as separate script has no measurable impact.
Gen 34 + inline formation (tanks forward, ranged back). 17 wins, 0 timeouts. Converted seeds 2,3,4 from timeouts. Better than separate formation script.
CATASTROPHIC. DPS-weighted targeting (damage/hp ratio). 50/50 result — targeting high-DPS units instead of closest scatters damage and loses focus fire advantage.
Melee focus closest, ranged focus weakest. Worse — splitting targets between roles breaks unified focus fire.
CATASTROPHIC. Kill-secure targeting (lowest absolute HP). 50/50 — targeting wounded enemies anywhere in range causes army to chase, same failure mode as weakest targeting.
Threat-count targeting (most-threatening enemy). Slightly worse — high-threat enemy often equals closest anyway, extra budget cost of threats_to not worth it.
Centroid-bias kiting (70% flee + 30% toward army center). Worse — blending flee direction reduces kite effectiveness, more timeouts.
HP-threshold kite (ranged kite when own HP < 70%). 17 wins, only 1 P0 win. Good individual improvement — protects injured ranged units.

MAJOR IMPROVEMENT. Timed push at tick 4000 (force aggression in final third). 18 decisive P1 wins, ZERO P0 wins. Disables retreat and kiting in late game, forces all-in push. Only seeds 11,14 timeout.
Staged push (melee vanguard, ranged wait for engagement). Same decisive wins as Gen 34 but 0 P0 wins. More timeouts — ranged waiting delays pushes.
Gen 060 + Gen 059 (timed push + HP kite). REGRESSION — HP kite conflicts with timed push. Kiting pulls ranged units back when they should commit in late game.

NEW ALL-TIME BEST. Gen 060 + Gen 053 (timed push + inline formation). 19/20 P1 wins, 0 timeouts, only seed 8 lost. Formation gives better pre-combat positioning, timed push converts all close games. Won seeds 9,11 (previously structural P0 wins).
Gen 060 + Gen 059 + Gen 053 (all three). REGRESSION — HP kite again conflicts with timed push, causing 5 timeouts. Less is more: formation + timed push without extra kiting is optimal.

key_insights: CLOSEST-ENEMY focus fire (target nearest to centroid, not weakest) is the best targeting — kills immediate threats, prevents chasing distant low-HP units.
Production building targeting (barracks/tech first) during push is a major improvement — starves enemy reinforcements.
GROUP focus fire (centroid-based, all units target same enemy) is the single most impactful script behavior.
Retreat wounded units (<30% HP) when outnumbered preserves army for attrition advantage.
Push to enemy buildings when clear or >=3 advantage converts timeouts to wins.
Conditional kite (only when outnumbered) for ranged units is essential — preserves army without causing stalemates.
Push threshold sweet spot is >=3. Lower (>=2 or any) pushes into marginal fights. Higher never triggers.
Timed push at tick 4000 is the BIGGEST single improvement since closest-focus (16→18 P1 wins). Disabling retreat and kiting in late game forces decisive outcomes.
Inline formation (tanks forward, ranged back for idle units) converts close games. Works because it doesn't interrupt combat, only repositions idle units.
Formation + timed push is the BEST combination (19/20 P1 wins). Formation gives better pre-combat positioning, timed push forces decisive outcomes.
HP-threshold kiting (ranged kite when own HP < 70%) is good solo but CONFLICTS with timed push — kiting in late game prevents the all-in commit.
DPS-weighted targeting (damage/hp ratio) and kill-secure targeting (lowest absolute HP) are both CATASTROPHIC (50/50). Any non-closest targeting breaks focus fire.
Split-role targeting (melee closest, ranged weakest) breaks unified focus fire. All units must target the same enemy.
Centroid-bias kiting (blending flee direction toward army center) reduces kite effectiveness and adds timeouts.
Separate formation script (formation_orient as shared) has no impact. Inline formation integrated into combat_micro is effective because it avoids command conflicts.
Wider kite range (3 tiles vs 2.2) is neutral. Default sqrt(5) kite distance is fine.
Tighter retreat (20% vs 30%) is neutral. No meaningful difference.
smart_fill is counterproductive on current FSM (FSM does own production fill).
Ability commands interfere with attack commands — DO NOT combine ability and combat scripts.
Per-unit focus fire scatters damage. Group focus fire concentrates it. Always prefer group.
Never split army for worker harassment — concentrated force always wins.
Base defense recall hurts — units stop fighting and lose combat advantage.
Scripts benefit the UNDERDOG most: P1 goes from 15% to 95% with combat micro (Gen 63).
Shared scripts amplify positional advantage — the stronger side benefits more from symmetric scripts.
Worker spawn offset was a major asymmetry source (fixed). Remaining asymmetry is map-gen level.
Map has ~80% P0 baseline advantage after all fixes — caused by even-grid rotational symmetry off-by-one.
Previously 'structural' P0 seeds (9, 11) were conquered by Gen 63. Only seed 8 remains as P0 win. Timeouts eliminated.
Less is more in script combinations — adding HP kite to timed push hurts. The best combo is exactly 2 features: formation + timed push.
